title: Not $10 Gift Cards
text:
I bought this pack of Starbucks Gift cards in 2019. Ive given them to friends and I gave 2 to my daughter.
My daughter used one recently and it had $6.52 on the card not $10.00. She had the cashier check the balance of the other card and it had $5.32 on it! She had forgotten that she had these gift cards, so yes, 2 years later decided to use the when she found them! Do they decline in value? And then both had random amounts on them! I'm embarrassed now to have given them as gifts! Friends receiving the gift card aren't going to tell you that weren't able to cover their order with the card you gave them!!!

title: Can Do It From Home
text:
Usually I either receive an Amazon gift card as a birthday and/or Christmas gift or I purchase them at a store. Then it's very easy to add the amount to my gift card balance using my cell phone. Well I was stuck inside for several days due to a blizzard and I bought quite a few books then due to a travel ban I could not go to the store to purchase a gift card.
So after checking my Gift Card Balance I saw that I had overindulged and it needed to be topped up. One of the options under Manage Your Balance was to Reload Your Balance. I had never used this option but decided to try it. It was so easy to do and I didn't have to leave the comfort of my home. I will be using it again in the future.

title: Convenient and fast - allows single purchases from multiple funding sources
text:
For the way I purchase this gift card reload feature is a major convenience. I have multiple sources of cards, and sometimes want to use two to three to make a single purchase. The ability to use a gift card comes in handy in those situations, so being able to reload meets my needs perfectly.

The way I use it for multiple cards is to reload from one card, then another until I have distributed the funding sources. It literally takes seconds per reload, and when I am using three or more cards, it can all be done in a minute or two. Then when I need to make a purchase the entire amount is waiting. This is much faster and more efficient than purchasing multiple gift cards.

You can also have your gift card balance automatically reloaded from a single funding source when the balance hits a low mark. I have not used that feature, but can see how convenient it would be for some folks who use gift cards the way I do.

text: I recently sent two of these as birthday gifts and the Mom got the loaded gift cards with instructions to let the kids have the tins since I could not bring them edible cupcakes. I suspect that the funds embedded in the cards eventually went to buying items for the kids anyway because she's a sharing person.  This is one of my favorite physical gift cards from Amazon which I enjoy sending.

text: The gift card works.  You apply it to your account and have the funds to cover a purchase up to the dollar amount of the value applied to the card.  You cannot add to the card, once it's applied to the account, the card is useless as a form of tender.  I personally have a tool to make guitar picks from cards like these, so I'll make a couple of guitar picks from this one and give them to the kids.  One of the kids loves to play his guitar.  The tin in which it arrives has a cutout with a plastic insert so that the warehouse workers know which card to select to fulfill the order, and that does not hinder my using the tin for other things.  I actually like to see what's inside the box, just like the Amazon staff I guess.

title: Pay attention if you purchase with an incentive
text: I like having a gift card balance from which payment is made for small ticket items and tend to uncheck the box for applying a gift card balance for purchases where a credit card payment is more beneficial, as when an extended warranty is offered.  Since this has no expiration date, I find that it allows me to save time when balancing my credit card statements at the end of the month, particularly since I can check my gift card balance and see exactly when and for what amount the payment was made.  When Amazon offers an incentive to reload is when I typically make my purchases, and although it has not applied the additional amount instantaneously, I have found that after a phone call, it miraculously tends to appear.  For this reason, I have to deduct a star from my review.

title: They Like To Choose Their Own Gift
text:
It's probably more fun for me to buy a gift, wrap it and deliver it in person.

It's probably more fun for the recipient to receive money or a gift card so that they can choose their own item.

You can never go wrong with money; a gift card is -- to me anyway -- slightly classier than money. And Amazon.com has a wide enough selection of items that the recipient can usually find SOMETHING; growing wider every day, too.

Once, or twice, a year (birthdays and/or Christmas) I like to give my friend's children a little something. And they seem to LOVE receiving gift cards from Amazon.com!

title: Love 'em - they really dress up a gift card!
text:
We had two people on our gift list who are getting gift cards, and we wanted to dress the cards up so they looked a little festive. These really do the job. They're not flat evelope-like things - they're made of thick, glossy paper, are nicely decorated, and can fit three giftcards or two plus part of the backing card. The cards are pushed up when you open the velcroed flap. Behind the card spot is a small space for a couple flat Sees chocolate Santas or something like that.

Absolutely delightful - I'm hoping to find birthday versions, as well.

I used to have to buy one and email it to myself.  This just makes it so much easier.

title: Card was invalid void not activated....
text:
I’d give zero stars if possible. After purchasing this gift card on Lightning Deal during Prime Days I will never purchase another gift card from amazon again. A $50 gift card for $40 wasn’t worth all the time I spent trying to get the card to work or
my $ back. What the problem was is still unknown. Amazon customer service didn’t help me as I was referred to Apple. After a lengthy conversation with Apple the result was the card wasn’t identified or activated. I was referred back to Amazon. It was a battle with Amazon Customer service (which I usually receive 100% satisfaction) to resolve this issue. A battle. It was an Apple problem. No it wasn’t. Finally since there was nothing amazon could do to make the card valid I requested my $40 back. Another battle after being referred to more people to discuss the problem. Finally after demanding resolution & speaking to the “right” person at Amazon, I was refunded the $40. Since no activation papers are sent with the card trusting the system applies. The system didn’t work with this card. After my experience I wouldn’t suggest purchasing gift cards. Amazon needs to state they will include activation paperwork with the gift card, Until I read such words I’ll never purchase a gift card again even if it’s a deal is too good to pass up.

title: These are MARVELOUS!!  (once you know how to scan the QR code!)
text:
I will ALWAYS have a pack of these on hand! It is completely worth the $2.99 for a 10 pack! That is $0.30 per card to have it readily available in a nice gifting sleeve and the ability to load with the amount you choose, whenever it's needed.
That being said, I understand why some have complained about the activation process with the Amazon App. To make it work, I had to download a QR scanner separate from the Amazon app. Then when I scanned the QR code on the back of the card, it opens a link in a web browser page, and I can complete the gift card loading process that way. It's easy-peasy to load and only takes a couple of minutes, but it may require a separate app.

I agree that Amazon really should get the QR scanner working for these gift cards within their app. I'm giving them 4 stars just due to the inconvenience of the Amazon App not working to activate them, and if they correct that problem I will change my review to 5 stars.

title: Who doesn't love some extra change? Especially if it tastes like chocolate!
text:
For my grandchildren, these Palmer Gold Chocolate Coins with gift card or money holder packaging will be "almost" as exciting as finding the Golden Ticket on Charlie and the Chocolate Factory! Of course, I had to open a package of the chocolate coins myself, which tastes like, well, chocolate. They remind me of the chocolate coins I used to get as a kid.
Though bags of chocolate coins are readily available, these are the first chocolate coins I've ever seen that open up to, hopefully, reveal some real spendable cash. In my grandkids case, they will be holding real money, gift cards, and probably some Monopoly money, just to make them a little more fun.
These chocolate coin money holders are very well-made, the chocolate tastes pretty good, and they are so much fun! These will make great stocking stuffers, party favors, and good behavior or chore chart rewards, etc. I will be making these up as the Treasures for a Christmas scavenger hunt.
I think these are yummy, great fun, and can be used in many creative ways. I would absolutely recommend them!

Olive Garden overcharged their customers for worst food they made. Upon arrival at a local Olive Garden branch in October 2014, my friends and I saw only a few customers sit there so we should be assigned a seat quickly. There were several welcoming representatives just talking to each other and one told us that we had to wait at least 20 minutes to get seated.

After the dirty and sour meal, we were overcharged and were forced to pay 20% service fee as tips. If it's a good meal and service, we might pay more than 30% of dinner price as tip but we had a horrible dining experience in Olive Garden. After all, we would never visit Olive Garden again because such a bad restaurant kept ripping people off. I have seen bad reviews on this restaurant from other people on other sites too.

text: I stumbled on this while looking for a graduation card and I am so glad I did! The graduation cap box With cute tassel is adorable! I could choose dollar amount for the gift card to amazon. I was able to write a note to the graduate which was nice! I also was able to ship it directly to the person (instead of getting it myself and then having to drop it off at the post office to mail) which was a big bonus! My friend and the graduate we’re so surprised and said it was ADORABLE! Also, since the dollar amount for the gift card is NOT on the gift card and I didn’t want her not to know or have to ask I was able check the box to email her a gift receipt with the amount on the gift card! I did all of this from my comfy couch at home! Highly recommend!

We've given every available tin and next year will hope for a new design. (Yes, carbon footprint exists with giving physical cards, but darn it we're old fashioned when it comes to Christmas!). Always a winner!

I really like having a balance on my Amazon account. I'm not sure why, but it's a lot more fun to get stuff on Amazon via a gift card balance. I got this gift card balance for myself, so it wasn't really a gift.

It took a little bit of time and navigation to figure out how to actually order one of these for myself, and not for someone else. But, once I found it, it was very simple to add to my balance. I like how I can pick any amount, big or small.

And, I'm mostly writing this review to see if I can do it, since there's nowhere currently on the product page that either displays what customers think or invites reviews of these awesome gift cards.

Pros:
+Quick and easy
+Pick any amount

Cons:
-Can't get other gift cards with this gift card

I also really doubt in anybody’s ever going to read this review since it’s going to be buried so deep in Amazon but in the past  I have purchased them gift cards for $25 and never received any response much out of them so maybe $50 will be enough to at least get a thank you I can only hope. I love them but sometimes I wonder what they’re doing :-) but as a bachelor I wonder a lot about kids. I hope this helps I hope somebody reads this have a great day be well

title: Always be cautious and AWARE..
text:
I had limited options and really needed to purchase this despite the problems I read here. My purchase was problem free but I also took the time to call the toll free number listed in information packet to check on the card balance. In my case the card was valid with funds. For this reason ONLY I gave it 5 stars. I however give VISA the brand “1” star because they have the POWER to implement changes that can eradicate all the issues around their pre-paid cards. It’s criminal and greedy to take advantage of consumers all because they have BRAND MONOPLY. Which basically means their name/brand carry enormous weight and clout that has afforded them a nice cushy spot as the leading source in pre-paid cards. Brand monoply allows for VISA something they otherwise could not afford to do because there are no other alternatives for us the consumer. There mindset is, “We don’t have to do anything but supply a card upon purchase...it doesn’t need to be dependable or reliable because we don’t have competiting brands that can match our years of branding and global recognition so we don’t really need to work too hard or break a sweat in providing quality service for our pre-paid cards...our integrity is competition based which means the less competition, less focus on ethics and integrity and more capitalization on being the monopoly!”

Unfortunately folks it’s a win win for VISA and crap shoot for us....so be cautious and aware of the game!...

title: What's not to like?  Except...
text:
I'll never say no to an Amazon gift card!

That said, I did have an issue with my purchase... The balance reload page offers several amount options (50/100/200/500), or a field to enter a different amount. I chose the option for $500 because I knew I would be making some sizeable purchases soon. I expected the balance to be reflected in my account within minutes, and the email I got confirming my order told me that's what would happen ("Typically your balance is updated within 5 minutes of your order.")

A few minutes later, it wasn't there. An hour later, it still wasn't there. A few hours later, it still wasn't there.

I contacted Amazon and was told that because of the size of the reload, there was extra verification needed on their end. This was surprising, because the reload page said nothing about this, the confirmation email contradicted it, and the amount I chose was one of the predetermined options.

So if $500 requires extra verification, maybe tell people that when they order.

title: Great to have--especially if you buy a lot of stuff on Amazon!
text:
I actually am a huge fan of Amazon Gift Card and also of the "Balance Reload." In the last 6 months I've been making a lot more purchases on Amazon than I did before (for a variety of reasons) and there are two things that I've noticed that kind of bothered me: A.) When looking at my credit card statement it always just says the amount, and that it was purchased via Amazon, so there are a ton of entries for Amazon purchases that don't really mean anything to me. B.) it's really easy to spend a lot of money because, well. . . you can find pretty much anything on Amazon and it's too easy to buy with things like 1-click purchasing, subscribe and save, etc.
Reloading a gift card balance means that I still get my rewards points for buying things on Amazon, but can also very easily set a budget for myself beforehand. Also, every-time that I buy something I'll be able to see how much I have left of that original amount. In short, if you buy a lot of things off of Amazon I would highly recommend using this feature. I still have some things that I buy through 1-click purchasing . . . but most everything else I use gift cards for.

title: Flower pot gift card holders
text: These are nice but they do not come with envelopes, just a clear wrapper. They also do not say how much is on the gift card. They do not come with any kind of receipt to tell how much is on which card. I ordered 14 for my kids' teachers, in different amounts, and I even ordered them in separate orders to avoid this issue. But they mailed them all together in one envelope, without any receipts or amounts. The cards were mixed together so I had to go through each one and compare the card #s to the #s in my orders. I also had to separately order envelopes. And in case anyone is wondering, the card measures 5" x 6.5", so "4x6" or A6 envelopes are too small (technically the envelope measures 4.75" x 6.5").  "5x7" or A7 envelopes should fit best.

title: What could be wrong with a gift card? Well ...
text: What could be wrong with a gift card? Well, Subway will freeze the balance on the card, meaning you can't use it, and there is no information on their site about what that means or how to fix it. I tried to contact the toll-free number on the card at least 10 times; after wait times of over 40 minutes per call, I gave up. I'm not sure anyone answers on this number. I also contacted Subway via their website every 2 days for three weeks. After threatening to contact the state attorney general office, I finally got a call back and the amount unfrozen.

title: EXCELLENT for Using Up a Prepaid Debit Card Balance.
text:
I was having trouble using a prepaid debit card for a purchase, as Amazon tried to pay the full amount of a purchase with it. The purchase amount was higher than the balance and I couldn't find a way to have Amazon use up the debit card balance first, and put the remainder on my credit card.

Solution? Buy an Amazon Gift Card for the remaining balance on the debit card. Then make your purchase, with Amazon using your gift card(s) by default, then your next payment method as is standard.

What surprised me the most was that I could buy a gift card for a nonstandard amount, which allowed me to use up the entire debit card.

title: Buyer Beware- Fandango is a Ripoff!
text:
I am re-reviewing this product since Amazon pulled the option (which is the same item: Fandango Gift Card) that had all the negative reviews.

Buyer beware! Fandango created a business to make it easier to purchase movie tickets online. The idea is good, but there are so many opportunities for things to go wrong and Fandango Customer Service is beyond terrible. The chances of getting burned by buying this product is very high. Since Fandango is a separate company than the theaters they deal with, you have to deal directly with Fandango if there are any issues with your tickets- the theaters cannot (and will not) help you. Fandango redeemed a $50 gift card we had and never issued tickets; when we called customer service, we were on hold for almost an hour (during which time the movie we were trying to see started). After having no luck on the phone, I filed an online complaint. It took several days for them to get back to me (in the meantime, I called again the next day and was able to get through after about a 20 minute wait). Fandango refused to issue a refund because their policy is that refunds need to be requested before the start of the show. I'd like to know how that is even possible if customer service reps don't answer the phone and don't respond to emails for days. They figure people redeeming gift certificates won't push the issue since it was a gift (not their money). There are hundreds of negative reviews about this company. Do some research before purchasing. Movie gift cards are nice, but I'd suggest going through the actual theater so that someone can assist if something goes wrong. Fandango just wants to steal your money.

As for Chili's, they were great for two things: $5 Margaritas on Thursdays and a rewards program that actually rewarded you. Of course, now that they have changed to Plenti for their rewards it is essentially worthless. But they do still send out emails (actually, almost daily) with free dessert or kids meal and "$10 off of a $30 purchase" offers. So if you utilize those it can be a nice discount. Oh, and the Carnitas Fajitas are excellent.
